title: Airbnb (ABNB) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Airbnb concluded 2024 with strong momentum, demonstrating accelerating growth across core metrics. The company reported nights and experiences booked growth of 12% year-over-year in Q4—the highest quarterly growth of 2024—with revenue reaching $2.5 billion, also up 12%. CEO Brian Chesky emphasized the company's preparation for expansion: 2025 marks the start of Airbnb's next chapter. This involves rolling out over 535 features and upgrades, including Guest Favorites and the Co-Host Network, which has already grown to nearly 100,000 listings in just four months. The company generated $4.5 billion in free cash flow for the full year, representing a 40% margin. Management repurchased $3.4 billion in stock and maintained $10.6 billion in corporate cash and investments. Looking ahead, Airbnb plans to invest $200 million to $250 million toward launching and scaling new businesses throughout 2025, with formal announcements expected in May. Despite these investments, the company projects maintaining at least 34.5% adjusted EBITDA margin for the year.
